# ZLAR

> ZLAR is a boundary between artificial cognition and worldly consequence.

ZLAR is execution-boundary governance for agentic AI. It is a consent boundary for artificial agency: routed actions cross a deterministic, signed, human-authored authority before they become consequence.

Primary pages:

- [Sample receipt](https://zlar.ai/proof-pack.html): human-first sample receipt with optional v3.3.15 terminal verification, manifest, coverage report, hashes, verifier, PASS output, and explicit non-claims.
- [Open Memo on Action-Level Assurance](https://zlar.ai/open-memo.html): policy and government-facing founder memo.
- [Boundaries and Non-Claims](https://zlar.ai/boundaries.html): exact claim boundary.
- [Archive](https://zlar.ai/archive.html): Founder Note, affected-person pathway, Sealed Mode, older proof pages, standards submissions, specs, and legal.

Claim boundary:

- ZLAR governs routed/intercepted action surfaces only.
- Safe Codex wording: "ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR."
- Unrouted shell/filesystem/browser/app/network/model-reasoning/final-text surfaces are not claimed as governed by the v3.3.15 proof path.
- /contest is not implemented.
- External non-Vincent verifier attestation remains prepared/pending unless state changes.

Interpretation rule:

Founder narrative pages explain the thesis. Disclosure boxes and boundary pages carry the precise public claim. If a page appears to imply broader coverage than the boundary page, read the boundary page as controlling.
